issue: [{"role": "user", "content": "Hi, please pause our subscription. We have stopped all hiring efforts for now."}]
subject: Subscription pause
response: You can pause a DevPlatform subscription from the subscription settings. For DevPlatform for Work, go to Administration > Subscription, click "Pause Subscription," choose a pause duration of 30, 60, or 90 days, and confirm. During the pause, existing tests and candidate data are preserved, billing is suspended, no new tests can be created, and no new invitations can be sent. The subscription will auto-resume after the selected period, or you can resume earlier from Administration > Subscription > "Resume Now." If you're using an individual self-serve monthly plan instead, pause is available from Settings > Billing > Subscription, subject to eligibility requirements. Sources: data/visa/hackerrank-subscription-management.md | data/devplatform/settings/user-account-settings-and-preferences/5157311476-pause-subscription.md

issue: [{"role": "user", "content": "I want Claude to stop crawling by website"}]
subject: Website Data crawl
response: You can stop Claude’s web crawler by adding a robots.txt rule for ClaudeBot on your site. Anthropic states that its bots respect industry-standard robots.txt directives. To block crawling for your entire website, use: User-agent: ClaudeBot / Disallow: /. Anthropic also notes support for Crawl-delay if you want to limit rather than fully block crawling. If you want domain-level blocking or believe a bot is malfunctioning, you can contact crawler-control@anthropic.com or claudebot@anthropic.com. Note that this controls Claude’s web search and browsing crawlers; it does not stop users from manually pasting your website content into Claude conversations. Sources: data/claude/privacy-and-legal/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler.md; data/claude/team-and-enterprise-plans/security-and-compliance/claude-api-security-compliance-enterprise.md

issue: [{"role": "user", "content": "i am a professor in a college and wanted to setup a claude lti key for my students"}]
subject: Claude for students
response: To set up Claude LTI for students, you’ll need an active Claude Team or Enterprise plan, LMS administrator access, and an Anthropic API key with LTI scope. The general process is: 1) contact Anthropic’s education team at education@anthropic.com with your institution name, LMS platform/version, expected number of users, and primary use case to request LTI credentials; 2) configure your LMS using the provided launch URL and credentials; and 3) optionally set usage limits and academic integrity settings such as Socratic Mode, Source Requirement, and Assignment Awareness.
